spec_match.rationale: Demo accurately models the pre-131 25-char limit and the post-131 no-cap behaviour for trigger registrations exactly as the chromestatus summary describes.

interactive.rationale: Slider drives a live before/after pair of verdicts plus a live JSON payload — real interactive surface, not a styled code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises a sliderable before/after demo of the dropped cap and that is exactly what ships.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path slider covers the 25-char boundary, but never demonstrates that the cap STILL applies to source registrations or that aggregatable_values keys must match the trigger keys — both are non-obvious gotchas.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex, no static-card pattern, no overridden button colours to mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add an extreme-length stress mode
openQuestions.detail: The headline of the feature is that there is no longer ANY length cap on trigger keys. The slider currently tops out at 80 chars which doesn't really emphasise that. Push the upper bound to e.g. 4096 chars (or a `give me a 10KB key` button) so the visitor sees the spec's true 'no cap' headline land.

openQuestions.title: Surface that source registrations still cap at 25
openQuestions.detail: The whole point of the change is the asymmetry between sources and triggers. This concept page only shows the trigger side. Even though there's a sibling `source-trigger-asymmetry` concept, this page would land better with a one-line inline note ('source registrations still reject this — see [link]') to head off the obvious follow-up question.

summary: Solid, focussed before/after demo of the dropped trigger cap. Could push the slider further to emphasise the 'no cap' headline and explicitly call out that the source-side cap is unchanged.
